title: Brotein Xcode crash — SupabaseClient init nil-unwrap (Secrets.xcconfig REDACTED)
html:
Xcode debug session — Brotein crashed in SupabaseClient.init(supabaseURL:supabaseKey:options:) with a Fatal error: Unexpectedly found nil while unwrapping an Optional value at Supabase/SupabaseClient.swift:174. Logs show the URL was loaded as bare "https:" (truncated — config parser didn't get the full URL) but the key did load. Side note: Yahir running on YGs IPHONE (real device, not sim). Both Supabase URL + JWT visible in stack-trace lldb pane — redacted in this summary. Same root cause family as the Secrets.xcconfig screenshot at 1:44 a.m.

TL;DR

  • Brotein iOS app crashes at launch on YGs IPHONE device because supabaseURL parses to "https:" (config injection truncated the host).
  • Root location: Supabase/SupabaseClient.swift:174 — host-extraction supabaseURL.host! split(...) force-unwrap.
  • Same week: Yahir already had "DeHardcoding API Keys" + "Safe APIKEYS" on the Brotein roadmap — this crash is what surfaced the bug.

brotein · xcode · supabase · swiftui · m1 mac

related.label: Brotein commit history — supplement carousel, premium-Apple-Watch onboarding, search overhaul
Brotein roadmap tabs — DeHardcoding API Keys, RevenueCat, TikTok, food-icon spec list
